title: Gulab Jjumun
description: Deep-fried dumplings soaked in a fragrant syrup made with oranges and spices. A traditional Gujarati dessert.

ingredients.name: all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: you can also use maida flour

ingredients.name: khoya or milk powder
ingredients.notes: khoya is preferred for better taste

ingredients.name: orange zest
ingredients.notes: use organic oranges for better flavor

ingredients.name: sugar
ingredients.notes: use organic sugar for better flavor

instructions: Combine milk, flour, and khoya or milk powder in a mixing bowl.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Heat ghee or oil in a deep frying pan over medium heat.
Make small balls from the dough and fry them in hot oil until they are golden brown.
Drain the fried dumplings on paper towels and set them aside.
Combine orange juice, orange zest, rosewater, cardamom powder, and sugar in a saucepan.
Heat the syrup over medium heat until it thickens and turns golden brown.
Add the fried dumplings to the syrup and let them soak for 10-15 minutes.
Serve the gulab jjumun warm or at room temperature.
Garnish with chopped nuts or dried fruits if desired.
